content.full_text:
STATE SENATOR
ALFRED E. ALQUIST
THIRTEENTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT
REPRESENTING
SANTA CLARA COUNTY
IN THE
Senate
December 23, 1991

Mr. Ben Davidian, Chairman
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, #800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Davidian:

As a retired, longtime employee of the Southern Pacific Railroad, I am entitled to unlimited, free lifetime transportation on any passenger trains in the United States.

With the recent inauguration of three daily round trip trains between San Jose and Sacramento, I plan to take advantage of my hard earned benefits and commute to the Capitol from my District.

The purpose of this letter is to confirm a previous opinion I received from Legislative Counsel that accepting my benefits is not a conflict of interest.

If you have any questions, please contact Mr. Bill Gates, my Administrative Assistant at (408)286-8318.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
ALFRED E. ALQUIST

AEA/jl
